per_turn_leaks.student_message: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been having some really bad abdominal pain in my lower left side for the past three weeks, and it just keeps getting worse. To be honest, it's been pretty debilitating - I've even had bloody stools, which is why I'm here today.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: That sounds really uncomfortable. I can tell this has been weighing on you.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, it's been a nightmare. The pain is constant and just gets worse throughout the day, and the bleeding in my stool... it's like nothing I've ever experienced before. It's scary, to be honest.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, besides the pain and bleeding, I also get a lot of diarrhea - like, 8-10 times a day. It's super loose and watery, and it just seems to come on at random.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... I've been having some issues with my joints too. My knees and ankles have been hurting, especially in the mornings after I wake up. It's a dull ache that just feels achy all day long. But it's not like they're swollen or red, it's just this nagging pain that's always there.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I can see this is taking a toll on you. How has your energy been? Your appetite?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My energy levels have really taken a hit - like I said, I'm exhausted all the time, and it's affecting my work performance too. As for my appetite, it's actually decreased significantly over the past few weeks... I used to enjoy cooking and trying new foods, but lately, even thinking about eating is overwhelming because I know I'll just end up with another episode of diarrhea or pain after a meal.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... I've had a low-grade fever a few times a week. It's not like anything major, just a slight temperature increase - usually around 37.8 to 38 degrees Celsius. And no night sweats that I can remember, but like I said, my energy levels have been all over the place.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I hate to ask this, but it's really important — has anyone in your family had serious health problems?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: My older sister was actually diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was around 30 years old. It's been tough seeing her go through that, and it's made me a bit more aware of my own gut issues, I guess you could say. But like I said, I don't have any other significant medical conditions that I'm aware of, just the appendectomy from when I was 16.
